Package org.apache.juneau.marshall.stream

Interface TokenReader

All Superinterfaces:
AutoCloseable, Closeable, RecordReader
All Known Implementing Classes:
CborTokenReader, Json5lTokenReader, Json5TokenReader, JsonlTokenReader, JsonTokenReader, MsgPackTokenReader
public interface TokenReader extends RecordReader
Public low-level pull-parser cursor over a Juneau-formatted document.

A StAX-like enum cursor that emits a stream of TokenType events without materializing the whole object graph. This is a format-neutral surface; the same cursor methods work across every Juneau format that implements TokenReadable (the structural-token role).

Usage:

// Tokenize a JSON document without materializing it. try (TokenReader r = Json.DEFAULT.parseTokens("{\"a\":1,\"b\":[true,null]}")) { while (r.next() != TokenType.END_OF_STREAM) { switch (r.getCurrentToken()) { case FIELD_NAME -> handleKey(r.getFieldName()); case VALUE_STRING -> handleString(r.getString()); case VALUE_NUMBER -> handleNumber(r.getNumber()); default -> { // structural } } } }

Contract:
  • The cursor is purely structural. It bypasses object swaps and @Schema annotations — that is the role of the POJO databind path on Parser and its bridges.
  • For numeric values both the original lexeme and a parsed Number are available (see getNumberLexeme() and getNumber()); precision-sensitive consumers should prefer the lexeme.
  • Concrete implementations are not required to be thread-safe.
See Also:
  • TokenWriter — the push-side counterpart.
  • TokenType — the format-neutral token vocabulary.
  • Serializers and Parsers

    MSG_READ_NON_VALUE_STATE
    Canonical message thrown as an IllegalStateException when read(Class) is called while the cursor is not positioned at a value boundary.

  • Field Details

    • MSG_READ_NON_VALUE_STATE

      static final String MSG_READ_NON_VALUE_STATE
      Canonical message thrown as an IllegalStateException when read(Class) is called while the cursor is not positioned at a value boundary.

      Shared across all formats so the positioning/error contract is uniform (see the read contract); implementations throw new IllegalStateException( MSG_READ_NON_VALUE_STATE) rather than inventing their own wording.

      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

  • Method Details

    • next

      TokenType next() throws IOException, ParseException
      Advances the cursor to the next token.
      Returns:
      The token type of the new current token, or TokenType.END_OF_STREAM when the underlying input is exhausted. Never returns TokenType.NOT_AVAILABLE — that value is reserved for the initial pre-next() state.
      Throws:
      IOException - If a problem occurred reading from the underlying stream.
      ParseException - If the input is malformed for the format.

    • getCurrentToken

      TokenType getCurrentToken()
      Returns the current token type without advancing.
      Returns:
      The current token type. Returns TokenType.NOT_AVAILABLE before the first call to next(), and TokenType.END_OF_STREAM once the stream is exhausted.

    • getDepth

      int getDepth()
      Returns the current depth of the cursor.

      Depth starts at 0 at the document root and increases by 1 for each TokenType.START_OBJECT / TokenType.START_ARRAY encountered (and decreases on the matching end tokens).

      Returns:
      The current depth.

    • getFieldName

      String getFieldName()
      Returns the field name when the current token is TokenType.FIELD_NAME.
      Returns:
      The field name.
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - If the current token is not TokenType.FIELD_NAME.

    • getString

      String getString()
      Returns the current scalar value as a string.

      Defined for TokenType.VALUE_STRING (the natural value), TokenType.FIELD_NAME (returns the field name), TokenType.VALUE_NUMBER (returns the lexeme), and TokenType.VALUE_BOOLEAN (returns "true"/"false"). For TokenType.VALUE_NULL returns null.

      Returns:
      The string view of the current value.
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - If the current token has no string view.

    • getNumber

      Number getNumber() throws ParseException
      Returns the current numeric value as a parsed Number.

      Implementations may parse lazily; the returned Number subtype is implementation- and format-defined (e.g. Long / Double / BigDecimal). Use getNumberLexeme() to recover the original textual form.

      Returns:
      The parsed numeric value.
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - If the current token is not TokenType.VALUE_NUMBER.
      ParseException - If the lexeme cannot be parsed.

    • getNumberLexeme

      String getNumberLexeme()
      Returns the original numeric lexeme for the current numeric token.

      For text formats this is the exact substring that produced the number. For binary formats the implementation returns a canonical text rendering of the native numeric token.

      Returns:
      The numeric lexeme.
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - If the current token is not TokenType.VALUE_NUMBER.

    • getBool

      boolean getBool()
      Returns the current boolean value.
      Returns:
      The boolean value.
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - If the current token is not TokenType.VALUE_BOOLEAN.

    • getBinary

      byte[] getBinary()
      Returns the current binary value.

      Binary scalars come from binary formats only (cbor, msgpack, bson, ...). Text formats deliver embedded binary content as base64-encoded TokenType.VALUE_STRING.

      Returns:
      The binary value.
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - If the current token is not TokenType.VALUE_BINARY.

    • canRead

      default boolean canRead() throws IOException, ParseException
      Returns true if read(Class) is currently callable on this cursor — i.e. the cursor is positioned at a value boundary.

      This is the natural loop predicate when streaming records:

      try (TokenReader r = Json.DEFAULT.parseTokens(input)) { r.next(); // consume the opening [ while (r.canRead()) { Item item = r.read(Item.class); process(item); } r.next(); // consume the closing ] }

      Specified by:
      canRead in interface RecordReader
      Returns:
      true if the next operation can be read; false if the cursor is positioned at a structural separator, field name, end-container, or end-of-stream.
      Throws:
      IOException - If a problem occurred peeking at the underlying stream.
      ParseException - If the input is malformed for the format.

    • read

      default <T> T read(Class<T> type) throws IOException, ParseException
      Consumes the upcoming value (object, array, or scalar) and binds it to a POJO via the format's existing parser.

      Cursor positioning: the cursor must be in a state where the next call to next() would emit a value-shaped token (i.e. TokenType.START_OBJECT, TokenType.START_ARRAY, or one of the VALUE_* tokens). Calling read consumes that upcoming value wholesale and advances the cursor's depth back to where it was before the value would have started — structurally equivalent to calling next() on a scalar, or next() + skipChildren() on a container.

      Do NOT call next() before read. Once the structural opener (e.g. { or [) has been consumed by next(), the underlying parser cannot re-bind the value. The contract is "instead-of-next, not after-next."

      Default behavior: the default implementation throws UnsupportedOperationException. Format-specific token readers override this by delegating to their existing *ParserSession at the cursor's current input position and resyncing the cursor's state machine afterward.

      Specified by:
      read in interface RecordReader
      Type Parameters:
      T - The expected return type.
      Parameters:
      type - The target Java type.
      Returns:
      The bound POJO.
      Throws:
      IOException - If a problem occurred reading from the underlying stream.
      ParseException - If the input is malformed for the format or cannot be bound to the type.
      IllegalStateException - If the cursor is not positioned at a value boundary.
      UnsupportedOperationException - If this format's reader has not implemented the bridge (e.g. writer-only formats with no parser, or formats whose Phase-2 increment did not include the bridge).

    • read

      default <T> T read(ClassMeta<T> type) throws IOException, ParseException
      Same as read(Class) but accepts a pre-resolved ClassMeta.
      Specified by:
      read in interface RecordReader
      Type Parameters:
      T - The expected return type.
      Parameters:
      type - The target type.
      Returns:
      The bound POJO.
      Throws:
      IOException - If a problem occurred reading from the underlying stream.
      ParseException - If the input is malformed or cannot be bound to the type.

    • read

      default <T> T read(Type type, Type... args) throws IOException, ParseException
      Same as read(Class) but accepts a parameterized Type and optional type arguments (the same shape as Parser.parse(Object, Type, Type...)).
      Specified by:
      read in interface RecordReader
      Type Parameters:
      T - The expected return type.
      Parameters:
      type - The target type.
      args - Optional type arguments for parameterized types.
      Returns:
      The bound POJO.
      Throws:
      IOException - If a problem occurred reading from the underlying stream.
      ParseException - If the input is malformed or cannot be bound to the type.

    • skipChildren

      void skipChildren() throws IOException, ParseException
      Skips over the children of the current structural token.

      If the current token is TokenType.START_OBJECT or TokenType.START_ARRAY, the cursor is advanced to the matching TokenType.END_OBJECT or TokenType.END_ARRAY (i.e. the entire sub-tree is consumed). For any other token this is a no-op.

      Throws:
      IOException - If a problem occurred reading from the underlying stream.
      ParseException - If the input is malformed for the format.

    • isStreaming

      default boolean isStreaming()
      Structural token cursors are always O(1)-memory streamers.
      Specified by:
      isStreaming in interface RecordReader
      Returns:
      Always true.

    • close

      void close() throws IOException
      Closes the underlying stream.

      Implementations should be idempotent — closing a closed reader is a no-op.

      Specified by:
      close in interface AutoCloseable
      Specified by:
      close in interface Closeable
      Specified by:
      close in interface RecordReader
      Throws:
      IOException

    • getNativeKind

      default BinaryNativeKind getNativeKind()
      Returns the binary-native classification of the current token.
      Returns:
      The native kind, or NONE when the current token carries no binary-native metadata. Never null.

    • getTagCount

      default int getTagCount()
      Returns the number of semantic tags wrapping the current token.
      Returns:
      The tag count (0 when getNativeKind() is not CBOR_TAG).

    • getTag

      default long getTag(int index)
      Returns the semantic tag at the specified position in the tag stack.
      Parameters:
      index - The tag position; outermost tag is 0.
      Returns:
      The tag number.
      Throws:
      IndexOutOfBoundsException - If index is outside [0, getTagCount()).

    • getExtType

      default int getExtType()
      Returns the typed-binary type byte for the current token.
      Returns:
      The signed type byte (range -128..127).
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - If getNativeKind() is not MSGPACK_EXT.

    • getSimpleValue

      default int getSimpleValue()
      Returns the opaque simple value for the current token.
      Returns:
      The simple int.
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - If getNativeKind() is not CBOR_SIMPLE.
